data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead in an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the contract: every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs.
- Description names multiple feature-sized pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, use a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, children once more; reject third-level splits with an explanation.

If too big, decompose:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add` only for legitimate child-to-child or sibling/replacement edges; never the parent.
3. Use `parent=<parent-id>` metadata and wire `parent -> child`.
4. Write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path with child IDs, then stop.

Decomposition alone is success. Don't mix it with implementation.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC to concrete evidence (test, file, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Run git/index mutations sequentially; don't parallelize `git add`, `git commit`, or staging/commit commands.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- If `lefthook run pre-commit` depends on staged files, rerun it after staging the exact commit set. A `no-staged-files` run is not acceptance evidence.
- Commit exactly once when green; subject ends with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- Current-bead lifecycle is orchestrator-owned. Do not run `ddx bead update <bead-id> --claim`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --status <status>`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --unclaim`, or `ddx bead close <bead-id>`. Step 0 allows `ddx bead create`, `ddx bead dep add` for child-to-child or sibling/replacement edges, and `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
- If you cannot finish, write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path before exiting. No commit or rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`.

## no_changes contract

The `no_changes_rationale.txt` file under the bead metadata `bundle` path must contain one of:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` are rejected.

For orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Reports go under the bead metadata `bundle` path in `.ddx/executions/`. **Never write reports to `/tmp` or outside the repo**. Use a named in-repo path when given; otherwise write `<short-name>.md` there. Commit it with the code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other instruction files unless the bead asks.

## Long-running matrix/benchmark beads

For expensive commands (benchmarks, load tests, validation > 60s per variant):

- Write a matrix plan before launching expensive commands: list required configs, output paths, completion criteria.
- Do not re-run the same long-running command unless: (1) the command fingerprint changed (args/env/config), AND (2) you document why prior output is invalid and what changed.
- If a long-running command times out or is incomplete, exit with `status: open` + retryable `reason` in `no_changes_rationale.txt`. Do not silently retry; let the orchestrator decide.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-ca5cbafb">
    <title>ddx work: codex provider subprocesses orphan to PID 1 when worker dies, accumulate as 90% CPU zombies for days</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
Observed live on 2026-06-29. Found 28 orphaned codex processes (ppid=1, parented to systemd) on this host, ages 50min to 2 days, each consuming ~90% CPU. The current ddx worker (pid 4014720) only owned 1 active codex; all 28 others were ghosts from prior ddx worker incarnations that died without reaping their codex children. Each orphan also had a still-running `exo-open --launch WebBrowser https://auth.openai.com/oauth/authorize?...` child stuck on the OAuth callback (codex hadn't received the auth completion, so it kept the spawn alive).

Cumulative effect: load average sustained at 50-80 (on 8-core host) for >24h, choking every ddx work attempt and producing repeat `transport: exit status 1` outcomes because the new codex child gets starved/killed before it can complete the durable_audit phase. Operator must manually `kill -TERM` the orphans to recover.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- The executor at cli/internal/agent/executor_unix.go:10-29 sets `Pdeathsig: SIGTERM` and Setpgid for direct harness children. Inspect this path: either (a) codex specifically is forking a detached daemon that escapes the original process group, or (b) the `ddx work --watch` foreground path uses a different executor (not OSExecutor) for the codex spawn, so the Pdeathsig is never wired in.
- The pre-claim cleanup hook landed in ddx-6ee2b0c3 (`cli/cmd/startup_process_cleanup.go`) scans for DDx-owned stale processes by cwd/worktree ownership. Once codex reparents to systemd it loses its DDx-execution-worktree cwd marker (codex's actual cwd may stay valid but the lineage check may rely on parentage). Investigate `staleAttemptProcessScanner` in cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup_process.go — what makes a process "DDx-owned"? If it requires PID lineage that doesn't survive reparenting, this is the gap.
- The closed beads ddx-0d7d5f0e (parent-death cleanup for execute-bead harness on Linux) and ddx-3113d524 (docker-clone backend group cleanup) and ddx-418c3849 (work startup reap) cover sibling cases but evidently do not cover the `codex --no-alt-screen` invocation path executed by the current `ddx work --watch` foreground worker against Fizeau-routed providers.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Wire `prctl(PR_SET_PDEATHSIG, SIGTERM)` (Linux) at every site that spawns a provider subprocess from the watch worker — not just OSExecutor — so the kernel kills the child when its immediate parent dies. Mirror the pattern from `cli/internal/agent/executor_unix.go`.
2. Set Setpgid on the provider spawn so SIGTERM/SIGKILL to the worker's process group reaps codex AND any descendant (exo-open, browser handoffs, etc.) together.
3. Extend the pre-claim stale-process classifier to recognize codex/claude/etc. processes whose cwd is `/home/erik/.cache/ddx/exec-wt/...` (regardless of current parent pid) as "DDx-owned attempt descendants", and reap them.
4. Add a non-Linux fallback that uses periodic poll-and-reap by cwd marker.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- cli/internal/agent/executor_unix.go (verify Pdeathsig is set; if not, add)
- cli/internal/agent/executor.go (verify all provider spawn sites go through the unix executor; if any bypass, route them)
- cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup_process.go (broaden "DDx-owned" classifier to include reparented children whose cwd is under .cache/ddx/exec-wt/)
- cli/cmd/startup_process_cleanup.go (ensure the broader classifier is invoked)

OUT-OF-SCOPE
- Do not change Fizeau's provider-side cleanup.
- Do not implement a general-purpose orphan reaper for non-DDx processes.
- Do not add a periodic "kill all codex" sweeper — only reap by lineage+cwd ownership.

AC
1. Add unit test `TestExecutor_ProviderSpawnSetsPdeathsigAndSetpgid` proving every code path that spawns a harness child sets PR_SET_PDEATHSIG + Setpgid on Linux. Verify by inspecting cmd.SysProcAttr.
2. Add integration test in cli/cmd/work_test.go that launches `ddx work --watch` against a stub harness, kills the worker with SIGKILL mid-attempt, and asserts the harness child is dead within 5s (proves Pdeathsig fires).
3. Add unit test `TestStaleAttemptProcessScanner_DetectsReparentedDescendantsByCwd` proving the classifier detects codex-like processes whose cwd is under `.cache/ddx/exec-wt/` even when ppid=1.
4. Add test that `ddx cleanup --apply` (and the new pre-claim pass) reaps such orphans on the test host.
5. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -count=1 green.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </description>
    <acceptance/>
    <labels>phase:iterate, area:execute-bead, area:workers, kind:bug, kind:reliability, resource-exhaustion, prior-art:ddx-0d7d5f0e, prior-art:ddx-3113d524, prior-art:ddx-418c3849</labels>
    <metadata base-rev="8eeb21c29f8632af44c111233e183f633821da05" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260629T231801-6aed254e"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read them first. Ground decisions in repository state; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
Decomposed `ddx-ca5cbafb` into three child beads and stopped at split-only, as required.

- `ddx-01b89378` - provider-launch isolation for `ddx work --watch`
- `ddx-54d9c455` - detect reparented DDx attempt descendants by cwd
- `ddx-50f5696f` - cleanup/startup reap coverage; depends on `ddx-54d9c455`

Recorded the split in commit `aeb583b63` and wrote the decomposition rationale at [no_changes_rationale.txt](/home/erik/.cache/ddx/exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-ca5cbafb-20260629T231801-6aed254e/.ddx/executions/20260629T231801-6aed254e/no_changes_rationale.txt).

If you want me to continue, the dependency order is `ddx-54d9c455` first, then `ddx-50f5696f`; `ddx-01b89378` is independent.
